filter_index.pickup_city: Sheikh Feroze Tours & Travels, Sathu, Barbara Shah Chowk, Srinagar

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow remains at higher altitudes in early July; most snow melted by mid-July to third week of August; snowfall can be expected during the second week of September

Light evening showers in early July; some evening showers mid-July to third week of August; rains reduce after end of August; Pir Panjal range blocks most rain clouds so rains are mild compared to Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand
temperature_notes: Early July: daytime 15-20°C, nighttime 2-7°C; Mid-July to third week of August: daytime 15-20°C, nighttime 4-8°C; Aug-end to second week of September: daytime 14-18°C, nighttime 0-5°C

terrain_challenges: Boulder sections on almost all days of the trek
Long descent on the last day with patches requiring care
Marginally longer trekking days hovering at altitudes of around 12,000 feet on most of the days
Trekking 7-8 km on average every day
altitude_or_weather_risks: Risk of AMS on any day even though altitude gain per day is not too much
Check BP and oxygen saturation at every campsite at least twice
Diamox can be taken as a curative course if feeling uneasy
Do not proceed further if not feeling normal after a few hours or overnight

fitness_benchmark: Run 5 km in 32 mins consistently for at least 2 weeks before the trek
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team 20 days before the trek date is required
A minimum of 3 screenshots of runs/jogs/walks/cycling must be uploaded
Monthly summary of the fitness routine must be uploaded
prep_window: At least 6-8 weeks of preparation is required
Start jogging at least 4 days a week
If unable to run 5 km, start with 2 km and increase to 5 km over 2-3 weeks

experience_themes: Early July: snow remains at higher altitudes, trek accessible, colourful flowers appearing on meadows, lush green meadows, alpine lakes still frozen in parts, light evening showers
Mid-July to third week of August: most snow melted, frozen ice in lakes melted, bright sunny mornings with some evening showers, green meadows densely dotted with wildflowers
Aug-end to second week of September: cold sets in, rains reduce, brilliant blue skies, deep blue alpine lakes, meadows change colour from fresh green to a different tone, flowers change

Lush green meadows punctuated with alpine lakes still frozen in parts (early July); meadows densely dotted with wildflowers (mid-July to third week of August); brilliant blue skies reflecting in deep blue alpine lakes, meadows change colour from fresh green to a different tone, flowers change (Aug-end to second week of September)
Snow remains at higher altitudes in early July; most snow melted by mid-July to third week of August; snowfall can be expected during the second week of September
primary_watchouts: Evening showers may occur; cross the pass early and reach next campsite by 4 PM; snowfall chances increase after second week of September making the pass uncrossable and the trail on the other side inaccessible; trek is usually stopped after the second week of September
Trekkers looking for snow trekking; trekking outside the July to mid-September window as trails are buried under snow
Boulder sections on almost all days of the trek
Long descent on the last day with patches requiring care
Marginally longer trekking days hovering at altitudes of around 12,000 feet on most of the days
Trekking 7-8 km on average every day
best_for: Trekkers seeking a short monsoon-season window with mild rains, wildflower meadows, and changing alpine landscapes in Jammu & Kashmir
not_ideal_for: Trekkers looking for snow trekking; trekking outside the July to mid-September window as trails are buried under snow
Trekkers who are not confident in placing their footing
